programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: The Fugitive

Meanwhile, Danny and Hawkes investigate the murder of a fish merchant and are led to a jealous friend who is resentful that his child was refused entry to a prestigious school

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The unit is tipped off about terrorist activity, and two agents are murdered during the ensuing investigation. Believing the information to be a set-up, the team tries to solve the case with assistance from a guilt-stricken Paula Cassidy, who holds herself responsible for the tragedy. Drama, starring Jessica Steen

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and the team are called in to look for the body of a murdered Muslim man that has gone missing from the morgue. As they try to establish a link between a top-secret residue found on the corpse and the victim's killers, their investigations uncover an experimental Navy tracking system and a plot to sell nuclear material to terrorists planning to make a bomb. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself in trouble with a vigilante committee when he arrives in a town where an unidentified stranger recently committed a vile crime.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Stone tries to make sure that a teenager does not get away with murder twice when he is brought to trial for fatally shooting one of his friends

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Alex's friend is targeted for murder when she threatens to expose hazardous building code violations. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Following an explosion at a cinema, Avery and her team go in search of a bomber who intends to expose the truth about society's over-reliance on technology. As the investigation progresses, Ramirez and Nelson find a website which features a video of the bombing along with a sinister explanation, and they face a race to catch the culprit before the next bomb goes off. Patricia Arquette and James Van Der Beek star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents try to track down the Navy employee responsible for uploading sensitive documents onto an internet blog. Discovering the owner of the website has been tortured and killed, they realise the culprits were trying to obtain the decryption key to access the classified material

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam goes undercover inside a federal prison, posing as an inmate to investigate a terrorist group responsible for bombings worldwide, and Callen is given permission to pursue a clue to his past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Jonathan is held hostage by a dangerous escaped convict and a bungling hit man, after he nearly runs them both down with his car. The authorities are informed of the perilous situation, which rapidly deteriorates. Nigel Havers stars

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester ropes in the family to help her organise a church fete. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle concocts another plan to infiltrate German headquarters and rescue the British airmen using hijacked German uniforms, but who will they fit?

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The delinquent pensioners join Barry and Wesley to help rescue a couple whose car is bogged down in a remote field - but the woman surprises them by revealing she has mystic powers and declares Barry is the chosen one destined to marry her. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unusual advertisement appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local gazette, purporting to reveal details of a forthcoming murder, including the date, time and venue. It appears to be a morbid practical joke, but proves shockingly accurate. Puzzled by the mystery, and with more suspects than he can shake a stick at, Inspector Craddock quickly enlists Miss Marple's help in solving the crime. Joan Hickson stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the mystery of a missing scientist, after the man's wife receives a series of emails from someone claiming to know what happened to him. The physics professor was last seen in 2004, on a train to Paris for a conference, and when he disappeared he was working on a potentially lucrative cold fusion project that had attracted attention from many large organisations. However, as the detectives dig deeper, a shadowy Whitehall figure warns Sandra's boss Strickland they should drop the case

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The American president makes an unscheduled trip to Britain, forcing MI5 to work with the CIA to ensure his safety throughout the visit. Tom and Christine are thrown together, and it soon appears there's more to their relationship than just work - but with each new threat turning the operation into a power struggle between the rival organisations, they need to keep their minds firmly on the job

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry befriends eager medical student Holly Farr after giving a lecture to a group of trainees and involves her in his latest case, the post-mortem of a woman whose body was found at the side of a road with the face expertly removed. Nikki tries to reconstruct the victim's features to help identify her, but Holly immediately recognises her as Fran Price - a former TV presenter and journalist who had fallen on hard times

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Nora dons a revealing outfit for a church pageant and is dared by Ivy to call Compo's bluff and present herself on his doorstep. Unfortunately, the shock proves too much for him and he is raced to hospital. Alas, he failed to make it through, presenting his friends with a dilemma on how best to commemorate him. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star in a special instalment of Roy Clarke's comedy, marking the departure of Bill Owen's character from the series

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: The barrister reluctantly agrees to act on behalf of a recently released jailbird accused of kidnapping his infant son, and gets caught up in a bitter tug-of-love battle. Returning home in search of solace, he clashes with daughter Kate over her growing independence - and finds himself forced to accept she is now a woman. John Thaw stars in the legal drama, with guest appearances by Dougray Scott and Toyah Willcox

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the killing of notorious biker-gang leader Eddie Chapman when his son's girlfriend brings them new information about his death. She reveals her partner must avenge his father's murder as part of a final initiation into the gang, but believes the wrong man is being targeted. Meanwhile, Sandra is forced to reassess her position at Ucos when a former colleague suggests she is wasting her career. Starring Amanda Redman, with her real-life daughter Emily Glenister in a supporting role

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The French Intelligence Service asks for MI5's help in catching an arms expert suspected of trying to sell Eurofighter technology to the highest bidder. It seems a 14-year-old thief holds the key to acquiring the information, but the operation slides into disarray when the government orders the British team to acquire it for themselves, turning an exercise in international co-operation into a web of lies and mistrust. Matthew Macfadyen, Keeley Hawes and David Oyelowo star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An armed police raid at a terraced house in North London sees two suspected terrorists killed, along with a police officer. Nikki doubts the officers' accounts of events after carrying out the post mortems as the forensic evidence just doesn't add up. The case then takes an unexpected turn when it transpires one of the surviving policemen is having an affair with his dead colleague's wife

programme.desc: William's secretary calls in sick, so Hester volunteers to run the office in her place. However, when an important new client shows up for an appointment she finds herself having to fill in for her missing husband. Comedy, starring Anton Rogers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Harvey tries to cut staff costs by employing illegal immigrants, and soon has the residents up in arms. Tom decides he wants to be as fit as Linford Christie, but faces opposition from a concerned Diana. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The wedding of Edith's mother Fanny to Monsieur Leclerc is brought forward to coincide with the execution of Michelle's most audacious plan to date

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As preparations get under way for Compo's funeral, Clegg is amazed to be given a letter written by the old rascal with strict instructions that it should only be opened in event of his death. Peter Sallis, Kathy Staff and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Three teenagers are shot dead while enjoying a picnic in a secluded wood. When a colleague of Wallander's is also murdered, he becomes convinced the two crimes are related, and sets out to catch the killer. However, the detective is faced with a lack of clues and an assassin who always seems to be one step ahead. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh and Sarah Smart

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the murder of a man found in an alleyway seven years previously when he is identified on a website covering unexplained disappearances. The victim is named by a woman whose husband hired him six months before his death, but neither of them had thought anything of it when their employee went missing - and seem to know little about him. Meanwhile, Brian and Gerry are distracted by looming job cuts and fear that Ucos could be downsized. With Robert Daws and Claudie Blakley

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The re-emergence of strike action brings a winter of discontent as firefighters, nurses, teachers, air traffic controllers, council workers and rail employees all clash with the Government. MI5 closely monitors the situation, aware of the inherent dangers - but when it discovers a section of the British Army is planning a walk-out of its own, it faces a battle to ensure the stability and security of the country. Reece Dinsdale guest stars, with Matthew Macfadyen and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of a member of the Jewish Hasidic community is discovered, and the team agrees to perform a 'dry' post mortem to respect the wishes of his faith. A forensic link is found to a group of Polish labourers, but Harry begins to wonder whether the victim's wife is hiding something, and decides to carry out a full autopsy to determine the time of death

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger encounters a young woman whose financial problems have driven her to the brink of suicide, while June looks forward to a fortnight in the Seychelles with Rod Jessop and Kezia gets to know Smithy better. Louis Drake complains of being attacked in Kristen Shaw's club and the uniform branch commandeer CCTV footage which reveals Zain as the 'heavy' responsible

programme.title: 3 Lle
programme.desc: Cameras follow actor Bryn Fôn on a personal musical pilgrimage. Bryn's life changed for ever after a Rolling Stones gig in Bingley Hall, Stafford in 1976, where he first realised he wanted to play in a rock and roll band. Bangor Uchaf is a place that inspired his musical career and where he began his theatre work with Bara Caws. Bryn also visits Dublin, where a concert at the National Stadium by Canadian musician Leonard Cohen gave him further creative inspiration

programme.title: Cegin Bryn
programme.desc: hef Bryn Williams travels to Île de France to meet Siân Melangell Dafydd and to discover more about the food and traditions of the area. He explores the world of chocolate and Brie. This cheese is famous worldwide, and creating this rich taste is a craft. In the kitchen Bryn cooks halibut, Dauphinoise potatoes and chocolate tarte

programme.title: Y Cosmos
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the sequence of events that occurred in the first second immediately after the Big Bang. Featuring interviews with Welsh scientists who work at the European Organisation for Nuclear Research (Cern) in Switzerland and leading experts in the field

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The hunt is on for a ruthless fantasist who has defrauded the NHS of thousands, and a cold-hearted insurance cheat exploiting the horror of the Manchester terror bombing

Away from the contest, Charlie learns about one of the bravest British Heroines of the 20th century

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Iris has a near-fatal miss on Vernon's farm, prompting Connie to issue a challenge. Finch has to protect his hidden treasure, Ellen receives a tempting offer from a visiting local dignitary, and Esther tries to hide her secret from the local gossips

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create classic puddings and explore the history and traditions behind Britain's favourite desserts. They also take part in the world black-pudding throwing competition in Lancashire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a dog-loving couple living in Oxford to find a new home in Carmarthenshire, and samples a regional delicacy at Britain's only manufacturer of pickled cockles

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David come up with plans to transform a concrete garden in Malvern with no plants to give it an Indonesian theme, spending £3000 to create a tropical jungle

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a saxophone that hasn't made a sound in over 40 years, a tarnished 80-year-old brass kettle and a shaving mirror from the trenches of the First World War

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey's arrival angers Ruby and Martin, although she reassures them she is only passing through. Ruby, however, has other ideas. Kheerat tells Mitch what Chantelle had asked of him and how she was trying to leave Gray. Ben struggles under police questioning as his story cannot be verified and Sharon grows tired of Ian treating her like glorified bar staff. Denise works out Amy has a crush on Isaac and tells him he cannot tutor Amy any more

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Part one of two. Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Having become close friends on their daily commute, Carl and Sally are forced to fight their feelings for one another, fully aware that the repercussions of a full-blown affair would be life-changing for both of them. And yet despite their best intentions, it seems they simply can't help falling in love. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in the conclusion of the romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Poppy and Elliot put their trust and compatibility to the test by dating someone new

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Kareem and Jahz dip their feet in the dating waters to try to fix their relationship issues

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A selection of luxury handbags seized from one are sold at auction, while Hackney Trading Standards target a shop they believe is selling fake electronic goods

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The specialists investigate a wealthy manager taking the NHS for a ride, and an organised crime gang who prey on the innocent in order to launder dirty money

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Host Anita Manning visits the Elsecar Heritage Centre in South Yorkshire, where Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Anita also pays a visit to one of Europe's largest houses

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Esther faces up to being pregnant and begins exploring her options, while Ellen's history with Dr Channing threatens their relationship. Henry learns the truth about Connie's past with Danny, John has flashbacks to his escape from France and Iris panics when her lamb goes missing. Drama, starring Dominic Mafham, Joe Armstrong and Becci Gemmell

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate Britain's love of seafood and the plentiful and diverse supply of produce obtained from local waters. They also explore the history of Billingsgate Market in east London, the country's largest inland fish market

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple with a £450,000 budget, who wish to relocate from Hertfordshire to the Northamptonshire countryside, closer to their family. The county was once the epicentre of footwear manufacturing, so whilst there Ginny gets her foot in the door of a sixth generation bespoke boot maker

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson transform an out-dated old garden into a luxurious outdoor space for Sarah and Tristan in Stevenage

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a wall clock with an extraordinary story, a fractured lustre bowl and a semi acoustic guitar. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: An investigation into cold callers making false medical claims to con the elderly and vulnerable, and the supposed back protectors that actually put motorcyclists at risk. Plus, details emerge of a scam that involves fake pedigree cats that turned out to be ordinary felines

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that once belonged to a man's grandfather, who served as a piper and a soldier in the Royal Scots during the First World War. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West helps to restore a Welsh crown of cultural significance that bears little resemblance to its former splendid self, clockwork genius Steve Fletcher takes on the repair of a quirky tortoise-shaped bell, and furniture restorer Will Kirk is thrilled to be presented with an enormous challenge - an antique wooden chair dating from the Carolean period

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Staff of London Ambulance Service gear themselves up for the fall out from another day of intense politics in the city, as hundreds of thousands of protesters take to the streets. Despite all their planning in how to handle the crowds, the team in control are taken by surprise by a problem no one had foreseen - the entire computer system used to respond to 999 calls crashes. Elsewhere, staff receive a call about a patient who is not breathing, and Advanced Paramedic Kev is immediately dispatched to the cardiac arrest

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Citydrop, the unit comes closer together. Meanwhile, Tally learns something new about Gerit, and Anacostia grants one last favour to Scylla

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: In the final episode, Raelle, Abigail and Tally graduate from basic training, and Abigail becomes desperate to prove her unit belongs

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Carbohydrates are at the heart of most people's diet, but with 63 per cent of all UK adults now considered obese, many experts believe people are eating the wrong sort of carbohydrate. Dr Xand van Tulleken sets out to discover whether they really are to blame. He learns how much sugar a selection of common foods releases into the bloodstream, investigates whether bread and pasta really are 'bad' foods and teams up with a Merseyside GP to trial a healthy eating plan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Merseyside Police investigate a carer believed to have taken a huge sum of money from the person she was looking after. Also, a fraudster's designer clothes are put up for auction

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The fraud investigators hunt a reckless hoaxer faking accidents for fun and a random road-side stop unearths the UK's biggest Blue Badge fraud

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value at Nottingham racecourse to take to auction. Anita also visits the Museum of Timekeeping, near Newark, to find out about a clock that kept millions punctual

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Channing refuses to tell Ellen he has been seeing Esther at the manor, and John decides to reveal the truth to Joyce. Danny threatens to hurt Henry if Connie does not help with his planned robbery, while Frank has a fight with Walter when he learns Iris is clearing dead rats from the barn

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King explore the history behind the boom in pub food, tracing it from the humble beginnings of bar snacks and chicken-in-a-basket to the birth of the gastro pub. The duo also recreate some mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunting couple find a country home with a budget of £700,000 in Buckinghamshire, and later visits a farm that produces high-end snail caviar

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to transform a Birmingham couple's garden, giving them a bird-filled place to relax with a fish pond and vegetable plot

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a fire-damaged silver vanity set, a secret agent canister from the Second World War and a wooden toy carousel. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat talks to Mitch again, wondering whether Gray found out about his lockdown conversations with Chantelle, when she told him how unhappy she was with her husband. But it only leaves Mitch frustrated and angry. Stacey has a heart-to-heart with Kat, worried that Jean will never forgive her for leaving. Ian does his hardest to avoid Max, blaming the bank for the delay in paying back his money, and Amy finds a way to still see Isaac on the way to school

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Shankly Hotel in Liverpool, which has themed party suites so guests can celebrate important moments in their lives. But with the hotel's online rating having slipped, a new general manager has been called in to shake things up, although his changes do not go down well with the staff. Elsewhere, wedding planner Ciara helps brides-to-be Trudie and Danielle, while guest relations officer Liam looks after Laura, who has checked in to celebrate her recent divorce

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can DI Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal O'Hanlon stars

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a tired teddy bear, an equestrian ceremonial blanket and a portable police crime office

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David visit Ibstock, Leicestershire, where BBQ fan Norman needs a place to grill and chill, and baking-mad Piper wants plants to add flavour to her cakes

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reaps harvest from his vegetable garden, plants up a seasonal pot and gives a masterclass on how to make the perfect compost. Adam Frost travels to York to meet a couple who took on 20 acres of land and filled it with flowers, and in Walsall cameras visit a garden planted with exotic palms and foliage that has made a positive contribution to the owner's mental health. Plus, another chance to see a collection of late summer flowering bergamot plants in Wales and the zoologist in Lancashire who has spent his life exploring the microscopic insects that live in the soil

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin takes on raw recruits and sets them on the road to being successful antiques dealers. Here, Dorset mother-of-two Lucia Foster-Found takes on chef Alex Koerber. Which of the two will triumph at the nail-biting boardroom finale?

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Having suffered flashbacks from her near-fatal experience with Stan O'Rourke, Sarah finds the strength to embrace her happiness with George

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter J, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A look at research into the lives of the marine mammals, which were long regarded as fearsome predators until the first one was brought into captivity for study in 1964, revealing facts about their profound intelligence, social interactions and parental instincts. The programme illustrates how contemporary studies continue to unearth revelations about the whales' behaviour

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The crew embarks on a journey through the Mediterranean Sea, whose coasts are among the most densely populated in the world, to explore the profound effect man has had on the body of water. Paul Rose and the team also visit the remains of a Roman shipwreck and, under the cover of night, dive the Straits of Messina, hoping to find and film the sixgill shark - one of the largest predatory sharks in the world

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher select items to take to auction, including a masonic watch and a sought after commemorative mug by Eric Ravilious. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the home of Britain's first Prime Minister, Robert Walpole

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Flower farmer Shelley and sweet pea grower Darren compete at the Kent County Show. Each of their displays are bespoke as they go all out to try and secure a gold card

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: One-time stars of The Bill Roberta Taylor and Trudie Goodwin set off from Glasgow and hunt for antiques around Stirlingshire, concluding with an auction in Harrogate, North Yorkshire. They are assisted in their endeavours by experts Paul Laidlaw and James Braxton, travelling in two very nice classic cars

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham prepare for a visit from the vet, who is coming to take blood samples from the herd to test for a chronic wasting disease. In Northumberland, Emma's six-month-old puppy Grey is introduced to sheep for the first time, while in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are dealing with an unexpected new arrival. On Little Rowater Farm in Aberdeenshire, a deer calf shows signs of being bullied by the herd

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Yasmin Khan and Professor Alice Roberts go in search of the lost remains of some of the burial ground's more famous residents. These include Matthew Flinders, an explorer who expanded the British empire's domains to the then-uncharted coast of Southern Australia, and Bill Richmond, a champion boxer who was born in slavery in Colonial America and fought in the American War of Independence

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Rob Beckett plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by rising comedy stars Jen Brister and Darren Harriott

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: A team of top doctors tries to diagnose a man suffering multiple seizures every day, but a cardiologist and a neurologist have different ideas as to the cause. The doctors also treat a young woman who has lived with a constant fever for nine years, which causes headaches, nausea and vomiting

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: A final round-up of highlights from the last two series of the motoring show. Featuring the team embarking on a road trip across the highlands of Peru in classic American cars, and Chris Harris putting an all-electric race car through its paces

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace consider some of the very best highlights from the show, from the most amazing dishes to outstanding challenges to some of the most impressive individual success stories. John and Gregg also reflect on what it takes to be 'the best', and how finalists and winners start to shine during the culinary rounds - in particular when in 2012 the semi-finalists took over the brasserie on a cross-channel ferry. Then the judges revisit one of the final challenges from 2008, when three celebrities faced cooking with Marcus Wareing - which resulted in one of the most memorable moments in Celebrity MasterChef history

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers return to their investigation of a curious case, a poultry farmer who buried the body of his fiancée under a chicken run, but claimed to be innocent of her murder

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David come up with plans to transform a concrete garden in Malvern with no plants to give it an Indonesian theme, spending £3000 to create a tropical jungle

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, the farmers prepare for mating season. In Aberdeenshire, deer farmers Isla and George wrangle their herd of semi-wild deer closer to the farm to tackle the challenges of rutting season. In the Lake District, Matt and Murray invest in some new rams, while in Armadale, Joyce Campbell's 600 ewes get stuck in the mud while being herded to the hills. On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham take their small boat out during settled weather to collect their creel pots

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin shows a cattery owner and a handyman from Clevedon the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: As Prudence, Delia and Carolyn prepare for what turns out to be a scandalous party in Sydney, Jack has to decide whether to confront his enemy head on

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary providing an insight into the lives of chimpanzees in Uganda's Kyambura Gorge, a deep, forested ravine running through the African savannah. For 15 years, the gorge has been cut off from the rest of the jungle, leaving the animals stranded with limited sources of food. The programme follows the 20 remaining chimps as alpha male Brutus tries to retain control of the family in its daily struggle to survive

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The team explores the Arctic Ocean, one of the most hostile environments on Earth, yet one that plays a crucial part in controlling the climate of the planet. Paul Rose and the crew dive beneath the polar ice cap to examine how the ice, the dominant feature of the ocean, is shrinking and how this could affect the rest of the world

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset and Powderham Castle in Devon

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Dairy farmer Tereza shows her prize goats and aspiring agricultural assessor Mark competes for recognition of his young Southdown flock at the Tendring Hundred Show

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Ex-athletes Linford Christie and Katharine Merry join experts Christina Trevanion and David Harper to find items to sell at auction in Buckinghamshire

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the third episode, Mary visits Ireland, where wholesome comfort food is at the heart of their cuisine. Here, she makes a recipes inspired by the Emerald Isle, including a salmon and fennel one-pot wonder, and slow roast pork with crackling and buttery Colcannon mash

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: In the third episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite savoury dishes, including a chicken and pink peppercorn pie, and simple, baked teriyaki noodles which are the perfect fuss-free midweek meal. Nadiya also demonstrates how to make a tear and share bread loaf with all the flavours of a pizza

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Isabella realises that her brother is determined to discover her secret. Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: In the final episode, Fallon asks Blayne for help as he could be exposed for attacking Amelia Scanwel. Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Action from the second match in the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby, as the sides continued preparations for the ICC T20 World Cup

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the safety of cosmetic treatments for the body, including a procedure designed to get rid of cellulite for good, fat freezing and fat heating, and the rising use of anabolic steroids for body enhancement. The programme also examines the psychological impact of selfies on body image and puts the latest weight loss technology to the test against traditional exercise

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to treat two more patients living with debilitating symptoms that have baffled other doctors for years. The first patient is 64-year-old army veteran Neil, whose body is covered in a painful rash that itches so intensely he has scratched the top layer of his skin off. The second case is 35-year-old nursing assistant Katie, who has suffered a dramatic decline in health in the last 18 months and has lost four-and-a-half stone

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if drinking cups of tea can increase the risk of cancer, and Danny Crates skips breakfast for a week to see if it helps with weight loss

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a wall clock with an extraordinary story, a fractured lustre bowl and a semi acoustic guitar. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson transform an out-dated old garden into a luxurious outdoor space for Sarah and Tristan in Stevenage

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thousand-mile journey from the Swedish capital of Stockholm to Abisko in the northern reaches of the Arctic Circle. In Stockholm, Michael braves a precarious tour of the city from its rooftops before boarding a heritage tram to get the lowdown on 1930s Sweden from an expert. Moving on, he finishes his journey deep in the Arctic Circle at a remote climate research station. He also has lunch with traditional Sami people in Vilhelmina and checks into an ice hotel

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best able to answer questions. Susan Calman hosts

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin shows a businessman and a reprographic manager from Norfolk the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack has still not spoken to Carolyn, so Sarah decides she must do something to heal their wounds. Olivia returns from the Riviera with some unexpected news

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In Nepal, an ancient tribe of bee-keepers has preserved a tradition of scaling a sheer cliff face swarming with millions of giant killer bees to obtain a rare type of honey. In this one-off documentary, farmer Jimmy Doherty risks life and limb to join these 'honey hunters' on their dangerous mission, as he travels the world to get a better understanding of the variety of flavours

programme.title: Inside the Perfect Predator
programme.desc: Documentary using ground-breaking computer graphics and close-up photography to examine four of the natural world's most extraordinary hunters. The programme follows a peregrine falcon on a mission to kill for its chicks, a great white shark hungry for seal blubber, a cheetah struggling to provide for its cubs and a Nile crocodile poised to ambush its dinner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Charles Hanson are in Margam Country Park near Port Talbot, where items include a tea caddy and a book signed by David Livingstone. Paul Martin visits Cardiff Castle to see furniture by William Burges

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson test their knowledge in a series of games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Highland cattle farmers Simon and Emma are showing their finest bull Merlin at the Great Yorkshire Show. In the Shorthorn category, Stuart and Gail are taking a big step and showing their livestock for the first time. Narrated by Bill Paterson

programme.title: The River Wye with Will Millard
programme.desc: Writer and angler Will Millard sets out on a journey down the Wye from the Cambrian Mountains, going in search of the river's source on the slopes of Plynlimon before learning how a local sheep farmer has diversified by turning some of his land into a destination for petrolheads

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The presenter ventures out from her home study, and her first stop is Stonehenge, a British cultural icon she has revered as a child. She has rare access to the stone circle where she meets leading cultural voices, including Turner Prize-winning artist James Deller and one of Britain's best-known actresses, Vanessa Redgrave. Mary also explores which cultural forms are the winners and losers of the pandemic

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the Wirral coast, the crew of Hoylake's rescue hovercraft is called out to an unconventional casualty - a porpoise stranded by the outgoing tide. Two hundred miles to the north another unusual victim of the British tides, a teenage wedding guest in suit and tie, is plucked off a fast-disappearing rocky outcrop in the middle of the sea by the Kinghorn crew, while in Cornwall, the Penlee lifeboat crew receives a dreaded call - a fishing boat, skippered by a fisherman the crew all know, is in flames miles out at sea

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A final selection of of highlights from the stand-up show, with Sean Lock, Katherine Ryan, Mawaan Rizwan, Holly Walsh, Andy Parsons, Lost Voice Guy and Darren Harriott offering their thoughts on the world of celebrity

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Having resigned from her job in PR, Julia tries to adapt to life as a freelancer and reluctantly agrees to help Amanda launch her new shop - even though she believes it is nothing but a vanity project. The opening is a success, but a careless online post threatens to destroy her good work. Meanwhile, Liz rails against the gentrification of the area and the loss of her beloved kebab shop, while Kevin reveals he has written an unusual children's book, which he wants Amanda to sell in her shop

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian dissects the week's news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. Joining him in an attempt to make sense of the world around them are Sara Pascoe, Sophie Duker, Jamali Maddix, Jen Brister and Dane Baptiste

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Comedy short featuring Bob Mortimer and Frankie Boyle. Frankie shows Bob his recipe for Lancashire Hot Pot, but runs into trouble when the show fails to deliver its quota of fun, education and social media buzz.

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An alternative nature documentary following the life and struggles of wild wolves as they attempt to hunt down a herd of elk. Narrated by Matt Berry and full of fruity language

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Comedy short set in the 1930s in which Hilda Stolf (Sally Phillips) visits her doctor (Reece Shearsmith) complaining of a rare case of 'high foot'

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian explores the history of cookery shows, taking a look back at memorable moments and mishaps from programmes featuring Fanny Cradock, Delia Smith, Mary Berry, Jamie Oliver and Ainsley Harriott. He also recalls the highlights of MasterChef and The Great British Bake Off, and reveals why innuendo is an essential element of a great cooking show

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: A new redevelopment scheme is launched on the edge of the city centre, intended to create nearly 15,000 new properties, but leaving many residents afraid their homes will be demolished to make way for tower blocks. Among those at risk is a woman who is caring for her elderly bed-bound father, who urgently needs to know how long they can stay in the family home. At the West Tower, interior designer Sarah works on a penthouse for a millionaire client, spending thousands of pounds on bespoke furniture

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: Ashley John-Baptiste meets a group of people working to clean up their neighbourhood, and Rev Kate Bottley goes in search of inner peace on a wire high above the cliffs of Anglesey

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if a daily glass of wine is a good thing and Chris Bavin asks if calorie counting is a sensible approach to weight loss

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Jonathan Scott watches the older orphans being introduced to a herd of wild elephants, and meets a formidable matriarch. Michaela Strachan helps break up the nursery herd in preparation for their release, but the move is disrupted when one of the animals panics

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a tiny waterfall tucked beside a glassy lake, surrounded by snowy majestic peaks and distant evergreens

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Documentary following three of the world's largest rivers, beginning with the Amazon, uncovering some of the latest discoveries, including the 'Blue Lagoon' which is home to a newly discovered species of cichlid. The programme also explores some of the Amazon's flooded forests, home to an array of unusual animals, and cameras follow giant river otters on a fishing foray on the rocky terraces of the Cristalino river

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer embarks on a journey across the country, meeting a diverse array of people and encountering exotic wildlife. He begins in Northern Territory, where he discovers how the camel is threatening the fragile desert ecosystem, then heads through wine country to the South Australian city of Port Lincoln to dive with critically endangered southern blue fin tuna. In Western Australia, he joins a 21st-century gold rush and ends his first leg in state capital Perth, where he meets a former binman from Hull who now has a house in the sun, a private pool and his own boat

programme.title: Chateau Chunder: When Australian Wine Changed the World
programme.desc: Documentary examining how winemakers in Australia redefined their industry, turning it into one of the world's leading exponents of making and marketing the beverage. Wine critics including Oz Clarke and Jancis Robinson reveal that good value, consistent quality and simple classification were key in helping to improve the reputation and popularity of the export

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: A decapitated corpse and drug-running bring taciturn detective Jay Swan to the remote, tight-lipped coastal community of Gideon

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Jay suspects the trucking firm is tied to the drug trade, but the intervention of an ex-military businessman also proves intriguing

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a tiny waterfall tucked beside a glassy lake, surrounded by snowy majestic peaks and distant evergreens

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder sees a chance to get some rest while hunting for a spy in the field hospital, and decides he is not going to let his holiday be cut short just because he has found an obvious suspect with a German accent. Comedy, guest starring Miranda Richardson, with Rowan Atkinson and Tony Robinson

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder tries every trick in the book to escape the trenches before the big push, including pretending to be insane - but ends up going over the top with sidekicks Baldrick and George. Final episode of the comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Palmer, with Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer embarks on a journey across the country, meeting a diverse array of people and encountering exotic wildlife. He begins in Northern Territory, where he discovers how the camel is threatening the fragile desert ecosystem, then heads through wine country to the South Australian city of Port Lincoln to dive with critically endangered southern blue fin tuna. In Western Australia, he joins a 21st-century gold rush and ends his first leg in state capital Perth, where he meets a former binman from Hull who now has a house in the sun, a private pool and his own boat

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Michaela Strachan accompanies the six orphaned elephants to their release site in northern Tsavo. Meanwhile, back in Nairobi, the four babies struggle to cope with the loss of their mini-matriarch. Jonathan Scott prepares for action as four much bigger female orphans are separated from Emily's huge herd in the hope that they will ease the transition in Ithumba

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he demonstrates how to paint a meadow at twilight

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological finds across the North of Britain. A team discovers clues to Scotland's first kingdoms, metal detectorists unearth a hoard of Viking treasure and a new housing development reveals a graveyard of Iron Age warriors

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.desc: The chef goes on a culinary tour of Australia, which he fell in love with on his first visit at the age of 19 and where he now spends part of the year at his home in Mollymook, New South Wales. At Botany Bay - the site of James Cook's first landing - he does some foraging and learns the art of cooking the Aboriginal way, before heading to the state of Tasmania. There he goes hunting for wallaby, samples one of the best single malt whiskies in the world and tastes sustainably farmed salmon that has become a national obsession

programme.title: Hyundai Mercury Prize 2020: Album of the Year
programme.desc: A special programme celebrating the twelve albums that make up the shortlist for the much coveted Mercury Prize 2020

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: The history of the written word, first exploring the hunt for the origin of writing, which reveals links between ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs and modern-day letters

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: The artistic legacy of the Middle Ages, when carvers created some of the most significant pieces of British craftwork, including the roof of Westminster Hall and the Coronation Chair, under the sponsorship of the monarchy and the Church. However, The Reformation saw many wooden wonders disappear for good. Narrated by Paul Copley

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as he demonstrates how to paint a meadow at twilight

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Michaela Strachan accompanies the six orphaned elephants to their release site in northern Tsavo. Meanwhile, back in Nairobi, the four babies struggle to cope with the loss of their mini-matriarch. Jonathan Scott prepares for action as four much bigger female orphans are separated from Emily's huge herd in the hope that they will ease the transition in Ithumba

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Emily's keepers consider sending her back into the wild when she becomes irritable and overly dominant. Michaela Strachan meets the newest arrival in the sanctuary, happy baby Jipe, and Jonathan Scott follows a recently released herd of 10, which is attacked by a rabid dog

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints an ice-cold pond surrounded by snow-covered banks, with soft hues in the sky

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Lean's epic drama based on Boris Pasternak's novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: The natural world at rest offers a constant source of calm and comfort, helping people to experience more being and less doing. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe talks viewers through the process and onto an immersive journey around the sights and sounds of resting wildlife all over the planet

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about Anne Hamilton-Byrne, a beautiful, charismatic and delusional woman who created an apocalyptic Australian sect dubbed The Family. With her husband Bill, she acquired numerous children - some through adoption scams, some born to cult members - and raised them as her own

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: The critic tells the story of Australian movie-making, focusing on the films that capture this idiosyncratic nation with drama, emotion and humour. This episode sees how Aussie film-makers gained the confidence to tell their own stories with the boldness of Picnic at Hanging Rock and Mad Max, the global success of Crocodile Dundee and Shine, the flamboyance of Strictly Ballroom and the raw authenticity of Samson and Delilah

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director of Shaun of the Dead, Baby Driver and Hot Fuzz talks to Robbie Collin about the movies that have influenced his career. His choices range from An American Werewolf in London, Carrie and Bullitt to Mad Max: Fury Road and 1930s musical Dames

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Sid has an app which flags local eco protests. He and Jas sign up to a bike ride organised to draw attention to climate change. When Sid discovers that participants are expected to shave their heads after the ride (to symbolise change can happen fast), he has an identity crisis: Good Sid wants to shave his head to support the cause but Bad Sid does not want to be bald and wants to pull out of the race. Mr Malone plans to leave school early to watch the Men’s Netball World Cup Final at home. He arranges for Martha and Ollie to teach his Year 7 class and ensures Sid will boycott a tutorial by telling him that his netball mascot ThunderBun is made out of non-biodegradable polyester. But Sid decides to chain himself to Mr Malone’s desk instead to avoid having his head shaved. As Mr Malone is desperate to catch the match on telly, he is forced to saw his desk in half to release Sid. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two – the veggie variety show. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny arrives, and it is soon clear they do things very differently. Love Monster has a night-time hot chocolate, while Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny drinks cold carrot juice. programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Having become close friends on their daily commute, Carl and Sally are forced to fight their feelings for one another, fully aware that the repercussions of a full-blown affair would be life-changing for both of them. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in the conclusion of the romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.desc: The team arrives in Finland for the European Cup. Last year, they came third, but Kid is determined to do even better and end her roller derby career on a high. programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Alison and Mike are trying to find a semi-harmonious routine with the ghostly housemates of Button House. The ghosts have found a way to make their new situation work for them, while the couple are working toward a new money-making venture to fund their perpetual renovations. But after a paranormal photo of the house goes viral, Alison and Mike spot an opportunity to bring money in more quickly by using the ghosts' 'talents' to create an authentic haunted-house experience

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that once belonged to a man's grandfather, who served as a piper and a soldier in the Royal Scots during the First World War. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West helps to restore a Welsh crown of cultural significance that bears little resemblance to its former splendid self, clockwork genius Steve Fletcher takes on the repair of a quirky tortoise-shaped bell, and furniture restorer Will Kirk is thrilled to be presented with an enormous challenge - an antique wooden chair dating from the Carolean period

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Staff of London Ambulance Service gear themselves up for the fall out from another day of intense politics in the city, as hundreds of thousands of protesters take to the streets. Despite all their planning in how to handle the crowds, the team in control are taken by surprise by a problem no one had foreseen - the entire computer system used to respond to 999 calls crashes. Elsewhere, staff receive a call about a patient who is not breathing, and Advanced Paramedic Kev is immediately dispatched to the cardiac arrest

programme.desc: Carbohydrates are at the heart of most people's diet, but with 63 per cent of all UK adults now considered obese, many experts believe people are eating the wrong sort of carbohydrate. Dr Xand van Tulleken sets out to discover whether they really are to blame. He learns how much sugar a selection of common foods releases into the bloodstream, investigates whether bread and pasta really are 'bad' foods and teams up with a Merseyside GP to trial a healthy eating plan

programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that once belonged to a man's grandfather, who served as a piper and a soldier in the Royal Scots during the First World War. programme.desc: Staff of London Ambulance Service gear themselves up for the fall out from another day of intense politics in the city, as hundreds of thousands of protesters take to the streets. programme.desc: Carbohydrates are at the heart of most people's diet, but with 63 per cent of all UK adults now considered obese, many experts believe people are eating the wrong sort of carbohydrate. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Andrea tells Moira the secret, Victoria returns full of resolve, and the penny drops for Liv

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh takes a look back at memorable exotic gardens created by his team, and the unusual twists the show has put on a challenge. The three projects featured in this special are a tranquil Japanese garden, a Moroccan oasis, and a theatrical Italian masterpiece

programme.title: Appropriate Adult
programme.desc: Part one of two. Drama telling the story of how serial killers Fred and Rosemary West were brought to justice. In 1994 the life of Gloucestershire voluntary worker Janet Leach is turned upside down when she is asked by police to sit in on interviews as an independent safeguard on behalf of a man they have arrested. Within minutes he makes a shocking confession of murder. From then on she is drawn into the centre of the investigation and a complex personal relationship with West, who tells her information about other victims that she cannot pass on to the authorities because of her obligation of confidentiality to him. Starring Emily Watson, Dominic West, Monica Dolan and Robert Glenister

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary explores how the Queen has responded in times of sadness and grief, as she faced personal tragedies and national disasters including the Aberfan disaster, the Grenfell fire, and the IRA's murder of Lord Mountbatten. The Queen also faced her so-called 'Annus Horribilis' in 1992, when family scandal, press ridicule and the catastrophic Windsor Castle fire tested her skills as monarch and mother to the limit, and, of course the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, when her slow response to the nation's grief threatened to destabilise the monarchy

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira confronts Belle, who plays dumb, Cain heads off armed with a bat, and Aaron is ashamed when reminded of his past

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam is horrified to learn that Daniel slept with Nicky, and Peter asks Abi not to tell anyone she made a play for him as he doesn't want Carla finding out. Elsewhere, Billy fears Todd is dead while Paul forms a plan

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, Virgin Atlantic's punctuality record is put in jeopardy by a captain's bottle of water, and there is love, actually in the air

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Peter berates Abi for confiding in Debbie and resolves to come clean to Carla. Daniel is determined to help Nicky but Adam is not convinced, and Paul tells Billy he's contacted the Gazette who are willing to run a story on Todd's disappearance

programme.title: Appropriate Adult
programme.desc: Conclusion. Voluntary worker Janet's relationship with Fred West enters a new phase when she makes a series of visits to him in prison, aiming to persuade him to divulge information about other victims and the participation of his wife in the crimes. As the case unfolds, she becomes ever more involved with the suspect - which ultimately proves to have shattering consequences for her at Rosemary's trial. Drama telling the story of how the serial killers were brought to justice, starring Emily Watson, Dominic West, Monica Dolan and Robert Glenister

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from the fifth round of the campaign from Thruxton, featuring the 12th, 14th and 15th races of the season. With commentary by David Addison and Tim Harvey, and reports from Louise Goodman

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The travelling chef visits Dallas, heading to Southfork, the location made famous in the TV series Dallas. James gets a tour before cooking in the grounds of the ranch. Once in the heart of the city, James goes to the Texas State Fair and gets and peak at the preparations happening for the huge annual event. While there he tries two Texan classics - the corny dog followed by deep fried butter. After a deep-fried, fast food hit, James then visits the arts district with a culinary stop at one of the major players in the city's fine dining scene, The Flora Street Cafe

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron apologises, Luke follows Victoria upstairs, and Cain offers Moira an olive branch, but it remains to be seen whether she will accept

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the lockdown, millions of people face joblessness, redundancy or uncertainty about their future on a scale unseen since the early 1980s. Robert Peston investigates the impact on the economy, with industries shutting down overnight, millions unable to work, and many more working from home for the foreseeable future. He also asks what can be done to avoid the mistakes of past recessions and assesses whether the Government is on the right track to avoid mass unemployment

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy fumes to realise Nate has been keeping a secret, a stranger named Meena arrives in the village, and Aaron has future hopes

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: The financial expert is joined by Angellica Bell for another round of advice on family finances, jobs and consumer rights, including the latest deals of the week and tips on making money go further

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Rose West & Myra Hindley: Their Untold Story with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The veteran journalist tells the extraordinary untold story of how the two most notorious women in British crime, Rose West and Myra Hindley, became lesbian lovers in jail. Former Category A prisoners, including reformed Mafia member Marisa Merico and ex-gangster Linda Calvey, tell Sir Trevor how Rose West revealed her murderous temper in prison, while former prison governor Veronica Bird, police officers and prison visitors describe how Hindley cleverly manipulated people in jail, even persuading a female guard to help her escape

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: Matthew Webb settles in and is introduced to the family but after a tour of his rubber plantation he is troubled to see that questionable practices are taking place. Joan sweeps him off to an amusement part with Monty and Ehrendorf, where Vera becomes intrigued by the newcomer, and soon he finds himself in the middle of a tug-of-war between Vera and Joan, battling for his affections. But very soon Singapore is hit by a war of a different kind. Adaptation of the novel by JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Georgia Blizzard and Elizabeth Tan

programme.title: Appropriate Adult
programme.desc: Part one of two. Drama telling the story of how serial killers Fred and Rosemary West were brought to justice. In 1994 the life of Gloucestershire voluntary worker Janet Leach is turned upside down when she is asked by police to sit in on interviews as an independent safeguard on behalf of a man they have arrested. Within minutes he makes a shocking confession of murder. From then on she is drawn into the centre of the investigation and a complex personal relationship with West, who tells her information about other victims that she cannot pass on to the authorities because of her obligation of confidentiality to him. Starring Emily Watson, Dominic West, Monica Dolan and Robert Glenister

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary explores how the Queen has responded in times of sadness and grief, as she faced personal tragedies and national disasters including the Aberfan disaster, the Grenfell fire, and the IRA's murder of Lord Mountbatten. The Queen also faced her so-called 'Annus Horribilis' in 1992, when family scandal, press ridicule and the catastrophic Windsor Castle fire tested her skills as monarch and mother to the limit, and, of course the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, when her slow response to the nation's grief threatened to destabilise the monarchy

programme.title: Appropriate Adult
programme.desc: Conclusion. Voluntary worker Janet's relationship with Fred West enters a new phase when she makes a series of visits to him in prison, aiming to persuade him to divulge information about other victims and the participation of his wife in the crimes. As the case unfolds, she becomes ever more involved with the suspect - which ultimately proves to have shattering consequences for her at Rosemary's trial. Drama telling the story of how the serial killers were brought to justice, starring Emily Watson, Dominic West, Monica Dolan and Robert Glenister

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The travelling chef visits Dallas, heading to Southfork, the location made famous in the TV series Dallas. James gets a tour before cooking in the grounds of the ranch. Once in the heart of the city, James goes to the Texas State Fair and gets and peak at the preparations happening for the huge annual event. While there he tries two Texan classics - the corny dog followed by deep fried butter. After a deep-fried, fast food hit, James then visits the arts district with a culinary stop at one of the major players in the city's fine dining scene, The Flora Street Cafe

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism
